subject: Statistics
title: Draft Pick Value
description: A micro-lesson that makes Draft Pick Value usable.

hook.text: The #1 draft pick is worth about three times as much as the #10 pick. How do we know?

buildup.text: Analysts track the career production of every draft pick to calculate the expected value by position.

discovery.text: Value drops steeply after the top 5 picks, then flattens. Pick #20 isn't much better than #30.

twist.text: Teams routinely overpay in trades for top picks. The NFL's Jimmy Johnson chart was the first attempt to fix this.

climax.text: Modern draft analytics help teams trade picks rationally instead of relying on gut feeling.

punchline.text: Not all picks are equal. Data shows exactly how unequal.

quiz.question: How does draft pick value change across the first round?
options: It drops steeply after the top picks and then flattens — picks late in the round are close in value
All first-round picks have equal expected value
Later picks are always more valuable than earlier ones
